title: NH judge: Concord man violated Civil Rights Act in assault on transgender woman
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Travis Lufkin, of Concord, violated NH Civil Rights Act by assaulting a transgender woman at her workplace in 2024.
Lufkin received a $5,000 fine and must avoid the victim for three years following a bias-motivated attack.
He pleaded guilty to assault charges, receiving consecutive suspended sentences totaling 12 months and a civil penalty.

title: Red Sox’ trade deadline plans ‘fluid,’ ESPN’s Jeff Passan says
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox's trade deadline plans remain unpredictable, with options to buy or sell depending on upcoming performance.
Boston's playoff odds improved to 44.3% after winning against Tampa Bay in Game 1 of a doubleheader.
Red Sox are reportedly not planning to trade All-Star closer Aroldis Chapman amid recent improvements.

title: Popular Nantucket pizzeria Pi is for sale for $12.5 million
author: Madison Lucchesi
bullets: Pi Pizzeria in Nantucket, owned by Evan and Maria Marley, is for sale at $12.5 million after 25 years.
The sale includes a 46-seat dining room, staff housing with three bedrooms, and a 20-spot parking lot.
Owners plan to maintain the restaurant's operation during the sale, with Evan staying temporarily to assist transition.

title: France’s World Cup squad bids adieu to Boston
author: John Waller
bullets: The France national soccer team departed Boston after staying since early June for the World Cup.
France moved out of the Four Seasons Hotel Boston, where they rented the entire facility.
France will play England for third place in Miami after losing to Spain 2-0 in the semis.

title: Matt Damon says he’s working on a Karen Read movie
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Matt Damon announced his production company is developing a movie about Karen Read's murder case.
Artists Equity's adaptation joins several productions portraying Read's controversial legal saga, including a Lifetime film and Netflix series.
Karen Read, acquitted last year in the death of John O'Keefe, faces ongoing legal battles, including a wrongful death lawsuit.

title: Steve Carell and his wife, Nancy, celebrated her birthday at a North End restaurant
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Steve and Nancy Carell celebrated her upcoming 60th birthday at Tresca, an Italian restaurant in Boston's North End.
Nancy Carell was born in Cohasset, raised in Acton, and owns a general store in Marshfield, Massachusetts.
Steve and Nancy met at Second City Theater in Chicago and married in 1995; both worked on 'The Daily Show.'

title: Some Saugus residents stunned by high water bills, with one nearing $200,000
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Saugus residents report extreme water bills, with one reaching $192,000, following meter replacements.
The town is investigating if new meters, old systems, or software issues caused the billing errors.
Residents have filed abatements and some face penalties; town to explore payment solutions amid the review.

title: Review & setlist: Bob Dylan was spookily stellar on a smoky night in Boston
author: Peter Chianca
bullets: Bob Dylan performed at Leader Bank Pavilion in Boston on July 16, 2026, alongside Brittney Spencer and Jimmie Vaughan.
The 85-year-old focused on his recent works, with limited performances from his early catalog.
The concert featured covers of Bo Diddley, Bobby "Blue" Bland, and Jerry Lee Lewis, integrated into the setlist.

title: The middle seat will become a table on United’s newest Airbus jets
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: United Airlines' new Airbus A321XLR converts middle seats into tables in Economy Plus with extra space and cup holders.
The middle-seat-turned-table feature will be available on all 50 A321XLR aircraft, with domestic flights starting this fall.
United's new seating concept provides additional elbow room and complements the existing three inches of extra legroom in Economy Plus.

title: Original ‘Gilmore Girls’ house is on the market, and it’s not in Stars Hollow
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: The original 'Gilmore Girls' house from the pilot episode is for sale in Markham, Ontario for $2.76 million USD.
The house, located at 31 Victoria Ave., was used before the show moved filming to a studio in Burbank, California.
The Gothic Revival home features original pine flooring, a modernized kitchen, and a 550-square-foot barn zoned for commercial use.
